description: This figure presents the temperature dependency of compressive yield stress (in MPa) for five Fe-15Al-based alloys, tested from room temperature up to 800 °C. The five tested alloys are Fe-15Al (+ marker), Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C (× marker), Fe-15Al-2V-1C (○ marker), Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C (△ marker), and Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C (□ marker). All alloys show a consistent gradual, monotonic reduction in compressive yield stress as temperature increases. Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C and Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C exhibit the highest yield strength across all tested temperatures, while the binary Fe-15Al alloy has the lowest strength. These results confirm that alloying with Nb/Ta and C to form strengthening MC carbides significantly improves the compressive strength of Fe-15Al alloys at both ambient and elevated temperatures, validating the high-temperature application potential of these modified alloys.
